_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform

question_results.text: Will Cristiano Ronaldo (Portugal) score a goal?
Will Lamine Yamal (Spain) score or assist a goal?
question_results.note: Biggest win. Cluster-A suppression validated again - real score/assist rate (~25%) beat the market's reputation-driven 51%.

question_results.text: Will Spain advance to the quarterfinals?
Will Bruno Fernandes (Portugal) have 1 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: The one miss on the two boosted floor calls. We pushed above market (0.35->0.58) based on his 75% historical 1+SOT rate, but Spain's dominant possession/service starvation broke his floor entirely (0 SOT). Small loss, but a real lesson: a personal floor built from n=4 games can still break when the opponent-quality regime shifts the whole game state (Portugal had far less service/build-up than in his other 3 games).

question_results.text: Will both halves have the same number of goals?
Will the first card be shown before the first goal?
question_results.note: Biggest loss of the match, and the biggest single-question loss of the whole campaign so far. Our base rate was built from only 4 recent games (1/4 hit). Crowd's 0.50 was much better calibrated - suggests the crowd has a broader implicit prior on card-timing distributions that a 4-game sample can't approximate. This question TYPE (event-ordering / timing-race questions) is now flagged as a genuine soft spot for our base-rate-from-small-n approach.

question_results.text: Will Portugal score the first goal?
Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will Diogo Costa (Portugal) make 4 or more saves?
question_results.note: Regime-coverage read was correct - Spain's dominant, high-SOT attack pushed Costa's saves into the high end of his bimodal range as predicted.

question_results.text: Will a substitute score a goal?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will there be 9 or more total substitutions?
question_results.note: The knockout-stage substitution-count precedent (9,10,8,8 from the last 4 games) paid off cleanly.

question_results.text: Will Spain have 6 or more corner kicks?
Will the match go to extra time?
Will any Portugal player have 2 or more shots on target?

key_lessons: Biggest win: Yamal score-or-assist NO (+38.48) - Cluster-A player-drought suppression, now validated across virtually every match this tournament.

Biggest loss: first-card-before-first-goal YES (-43.96) - a novel event-ordering question type where our n=4 base rate badly underperformed the crowd's 0.50. New flagged soft spot: timing-race/event-ordering questions need either a much larger historical sample or explicit deference to the crowd rather than a thin base rate.

Secondary miss: Bruno Fernandes 1+ SOT NO (-4.77) - the only case this tournament where 'trust the player's own floor over the market' backfired, because Spain's suffocating possession changed Portugal's entire service pattern, not just Bruno's individual form. Floor/ceiling patterns from a player's own history still need an opponent-quality regime check, in both directions (suppression AND boosting).
Diogo Costa 4+ saves (+2.62) confirmed the regime-coverage read: bimodal history correctly identified Spain as a 'high-saves' trigger.
13/15 beat crowd, +85.41 total - one of the strongest results of the tournament, in the same tier as Brazil-Norway and France-Paraguay.
